entries.statement: Ability to conduct risk assessments and analyses of systems, processes, and operational environments to identify, evaluate, and prioritize compliance-related and operational risks.
Ability to develop measurement strategies, metrics, and monitoring procedures that track organizational risk status, exposure, and management effectiveness.
Ability to design organizational risk management frameworks, governance structures, policies, and procedures that operationalize risk management strategy.
Ability to develop threat models, analyze threat capabilities and behaviors, and assess threat-to-capability alignment and tactical risk implications.
Ability to communicate risk assessment findings, risk status, and mitigation strategies to executive leadership and board-level audiences in business impact language.
Ability to assess, manage, and monitor compliance and performance risks throughout the AI system lifecycle, including risk identification, mitigation planning, and governance.
Ability to develop, train, validate, and evaluate machine learning models, assessing model performance, generalizability, and appropriateness for intended use cases.
Ability to assess system vulnerabilities, threat exploitability, consequences of compromise, and threat-to-capability alignment to evaluate vulnerability risk severity.
Ability to design and execute comprehensive test protocols for high-risk AI systems, including identification of risks, selection of appropriate mitigation measures, and test strategy.
Ability to design and implement post-market monitoring systems for AI applications, including performance data collection, analysis, and serious incident investigation and reporting.
Ability to monitor AI system performance and behavior against specifications, identify emerging risks and performance anomalies, and assess feedback and measurement results for risk implications.
Ability to assess and manage risks from third-party AI systems, vendors, and supply chain dependencies, including monitoring third-party compliance and developing contingency strategies.
Ability to analyze organizational systems and technology architectures to identify architectural risk, integration risks, and system-level risk implications.
Ability to design and implement risk response strategies, including risk mitigation measures, controls implementation, and remediation activities.
Ability to develop and maintain risk knowledge management systems, including risk documentation, lessons learned repositories, and organizational risk intelligence.
Ability to analyze intelligence from multiple sources to understand threat contexts, adversary capabilities, and implications for organizational risk posture.
